summary: Edge-walk wiring landed cleanly. NeighborhoodScene.js (east-edge sensor at lines 169-175 + import at line 43), DryWashScene.js (init(data) hook at lines 73-75 + create() override at lines 85-91 + imports at lines 31-35), seamlessTraversal.js (reverse adjacency entry DryWashScene.west → NeighborhoodScene.east appended at lines 89-93). Both access paths to DryWashScene coexist: world-map travel (entryEdge absent → applySeamlessEntry no-ops, original spawn) and edge-walk east from Neighborhood (entryEdge='west' → repositioned at west edge with preserved velocity).
review_method: Native code-quality-reviewer dispatch SKIPPED per session-scoped protocol. Orchestrator ran the verification checklist directly.

low.summary: Worker added a JSDoc comment on DryWashScene.js:67 mentioning `scene.start(key, data)` for documentation purposes. This caused a transient false positive in the orchestrator's call-site grep (count was 9, baseline was 8). Comment-excluded grep restored to 8 — no regression. The documentation is appropriate; flagging only so future verifications use a comment-aware grep pattern.

DryWash east edge intentionally left unmapped (the lore-side locked far-side trail isn't a real scene yet). performSeamlessTransition's safe-fail (console.warn + no-op) handles this correctly. North/south edges on both scenes are unmapped — same safe-fail. Worker correctly noted this design choice in receipt.

Worker chose to stash init data in `this._initData` rather than apply it directly in init() — defers application until create() runs because create() needs to construct the player first via super.create(). Sound design — Phaser's init/preload/create lifecycle requires this pattern.
